title: alchemilla hoppeana subsp. alpigena
intro: Alchemilla hoppeana subsp. alpigena, also known as Alpine Lady's Mantle, is a subspecies of the alpine lady's mantle plant, a perennial herb native to the European Alps. The plant has been used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Alchemilla hoppeana subsp. alpigena include its ability to soothe skin irritations and reduce swelling, making it a potential remedy for conditions such as eczema and minor wounds. The plant can be prepared as a herbal remedy in the form of infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be applied topically to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
